title: Origins: Verdant Crown
description: A symbiotic peacekeeper left to rust away in the ruins of the Verdant Kingdom. It excels at mitigating conflict with powerful crowd control abilities and a 'safety cannon'.
body:
_"...…we remain, and they do not. My first brother, the Verdant crown, was designed to be necessarily capable of enforcing peace and compliance, in line with the values Verdancy had come to symbolise post-standardization. It was publicly beloved by Verdant citizens, due to its competence in imposing such positive outlooks. However…"_

🟢 Restraint: Targets restrained by the Verdant crown would be significantly slowed, with both their damage dealt and damage taken halved. In this state, targets would spread their restraint to nearby entities, but would be completely freed if the crown was not in close proximity.

🟩 HLSC: The hard light safety cannon implanted in the Verdant crown's body allowed it to restrain threats at a distance. The cannon had to be charged for a considerable amount of time before it could fire, a process that would rapidly drain the crown's internal charge.

🟩 Sanction: In order to selectively restrain threats, the Verdant crown was able to sanction unrestrained entities. Sanctioned entities could not be restrained by any source other than a direct HLSC shot, which was the only way to lift a sanction.

🟩 Restraint Drone: The crown was capable of projecting drones that, while energized, would act as conduits to keep entities restrained even while the crown was out of their range. When first created, a drone would be automatically sanctioned. To remove this sanction and energize the drone, the crown had to shoot them with the HLSC and provide 160 post-standard experience units (PSX).

🟢 Symbiotic Sustainability: The hard light projectors that powered the Verdant crown could only be recharged by siphoning energy from nearby restrained entities. When at maximum energy, the crown's 'safety inhibitors' would reduce its damage output by 25%. When below maximum charge, these inhibitors would deactivate, but the crown would be completely unable to heal.

🟢 Verdant Roots: After its termination, the Verdant crown was left to rust in the buried ruins of an ancient village, awaiting the day it could awaken once more.

🟢 Pearlescent Archives: All three crowns possessed the ability to interface with the Pearlescent Archives from anywhere within the Pearlescent realm, provided they had a suitable antenna.

**THIS ORIGIN INCLUDES A CUSTOM MODEL FOR THE CUSTOMIZABLE PLAYER MODELS MOD MADE BY @SWONKII ON DISCORD**
